title: Thomas N.T. Sutton
content:
Practice Group Leader, Litigation | Partner
People.ContactEmail tsutton@mccarthy.ca
- Prince Edward Island, 2018
- University of Detroit Mercy
- University of Windsor
Drawing on three decades of experience resolving complex commercial, regulatory and professional liability disputes, Tom crafts decisive, innovative strategies that protect clients’ interests and deliver results. He approaches disputes as business‑critical events, aligning legal strategy with clients’ broader commercial, reputational and operational objectives.
As head of the largest corporate litigation and dispute resolution practice in Canada, Tom Sutton leads a deeply experienced, multidisciplinary team handling the full spectrum of disputes facing today’s businesses and institutions. Tom is responsible for setting the strategic direction of the group and ensuring that clients benefit from practical, client‑focused advocacy that is as commercially grounded as it is technically excellent.
Under Tom’s leadership, the group advises a broad and diverse client base across virtually every major industry, reflecting the wide range of commercial, contractual, regulatory, governance, and professional liability disputes the team handles.
Tom brings enthusiasm, energy, and intellectual curiosity to his practice. He has established and built his legal career with McCarthy Tétrault and remains deeply engaged in understanding his clients’ businesses and challenges. He is widely respected for his ability to tailor creative strategies, and for steering complex, demanding disputes toward practical resolutions while keeping outcomes front of mind and bringing disciplined judgment to when to advance, resolve or reframe a dispute.
Regularly acting as lead counsel in international and domestic arbitrations, Tom draws from his academic background in international relations and law, including graduate studies and experience in the United Kingdom through the University of Detroit Mercy London Law Programme. He regularly appears before arbitral tribunals and all levels of Canadian courts, including the Supreme Court of Canada, in a wide variety of high‑stakes commercial disputes. Tom is consistently recognized as a leading lawyer in professional liability litigation, an area that can be among the most challenging to litigate at trial.

#### Driving innovation in litigation
Tom has long been at the forefront of innovation in litigation. An early adopter of legal technology, he led the committee responsible for developing the Firm’s original e‑Discovery and litigation support platform. Today, he continues to collaborate closely with the firm’s MT>3 division and other internal teams to design and deploy technology‑enabled solutions that improve efficiency, insight, and value for clients. His approach combines strategic creativity with practical execution, helping clients navigate increasingly complex disputes in a data‑driven and cost‑effective way.
Tom is also a trusted commentator on the intersection of business, litigation, and technology. He has been featured by major Canadian media outlets, including CBC , The Globe and Mail , National Post , Canadian Lawyer and The Lawyers Weekly , offering insight on how innovation is reshaping dispute resolution.

#### Teaching, mentorship, and community engagement
A committed educator and mentor, Tom has taught advocacy‑focused courses at Queen’s University Faculty of Law and the University of Windsor Faculty of Law, and has also lectured at McMaster University’s DeGroote School of Business. He is a frequent speaker and program leader for professional organizations including The Advocates’ Society, Osgoode Hall Law School, the Law Society of Ontario, the Law Society of Prince Edward Island, and the Canadian Bar Association, and he serves on the LSPEI’s Continuing Professional Education Sub‑Committee.
Beyond his practice, Tom generously shares his time and expertise with his community and has served as a director for several charitable organizations, often leading governance and restructuring initiatives. A member of the firm’s Board of Partners from 2018 to 2020, Tom was a key contributor to the firm’s strategy and leadership.
